 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2ukh","title":"TEST: Crypto Primitives Unit Tests (Ed25519, X25519, HPKE, AEAD, COSE)","description":"# TEST: Crypto Primitives Unit Tests


## Goal
Prove crypto primitives and encodings are correct via golden vectors + negative tests (Ed25519/X25519/HPKE/AEAD/COSE) without panics or side-channels in logs.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for all cryptographic primitives used in FCP2.

## Test Categories

### Ed25519 Signatures
- [ ] Sign/verify roundtrip
- [ ] Verify with wrong key fails
- [ ] Verify with tampered message fails
- [ ] Signature malleability checks
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 8032

### X25519 Key Exchange
- [ ] ECDH produces same shared secret both ways
- [ ] Reject low-order points
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 7748

### HPKE Sealing (RFC 9180)
- [ ] Seal/unseal roundtrip
- [ ] Unseal with wrong key fails
- [ ] AAD binding is enforced
- [ ] Multiple recipients (sealed to multiple keys)
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 9180

### HKDF-SHA256
- [ ] Extract/expand produces correct output
- [ ] Context/info binding changes output
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 5869

### ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD
- [ ] Encrypt/decrypt roundtrip
- [ ] Tampered ciphertext fails authentication
- [ ] Tampered AAD fails authentication
- [ ] Nonce reuse detection (if stateful)
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 8439

### XChaCha20-Poly1305 (if used)
- [ ] Extended nonce handling
- [ ] Same test matrix as ChaCha20-Poly1305

### BLAKE3 Hashing
- [ ] Hash produces expected output
- [ ] Keyed MAC mode
- [ ] Incremental hashing
- [ ] Test vectors from BLAKE3 spec

### COSE_Sign1 Helpers
- [ ] Create/verify COSE_Sign1 structure
- [ ] Protected headers in AAD
- [ ] KID extraction
- [ ] Invalid COSE structures rejected
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 9052

### KID (Key ID) Generation
- [ ] KID format is stable
- [ ] Same key produces same KID
- [ ] Different keys produce different KIDs
- [ ] KID truncation is correct (8 bytes)

## Golden Vectors
Each category MUST have golden vectors:
- Input bytes (hex)
- Expected output bytes (hex)
- Source reference (RFC, test suite, etc.)

## Fuzz Targets
- COSE structure parsing
- HPKE unsealing (malformed inputs)
- Signature verification (malformed signatures)

## Logging Requirements
All tests emit structured JSON logs with:
- `test_name`
- `primitive` (ed25519, x25519, etc.)
- `operation` (sign, verify, seal, etc.)
- `result` (pass/fail)
- `vector_source` (RFC reference or "generated")

## Acceptance Criteria
- All crypto operations have test coverage
- Golden vectors exist and match
- Fuzz targets run without panics
- No secret material in test logs

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2up","title":"fcp.zendesk: Analytics and Reporting","description":"# fcp.zendesk: Analytics and Reporting (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose Zendesk reporting surfaces so agents can:
- summarize support volume/trends
- compute response time and backlog metrics
- track CSAT/quality signals (if available)

## Scope
- Fetch aggregate metrics over time windows:
  - ticket volume by category
  - first response time
  - resolution time
  - backlog and queue depth
- Optional: agent workload metrics (aggregates; avoid raw agent identity exposure unless policy allows).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `zendesk.analytics.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Prefer aggregates; avoid raw ticket bodies.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `zendesk.analytics.summary`
  - `zendesk.analytics.time_series`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - time window handling
  - unit correctness (ms vs seconds)

## Acceptance criteria
- Metrics are stable, correctly unit-labeled, and safe for logging.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-2vvy","title":"[FCP2] InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse: Schema Validation, Holder Proof, Binding Rules","description":"# [FCP2] InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse: Schema Validation, Holder Proof, Binding Rules

## Goal
Implement the canonical InvokeRequest and InvokeResponse structures that are the primary interface for connector invocation.

## Why This Deserves Its Own Bead
The spec defines detailed requirements for InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse that span multiple concerns:
- capability token embedding and holder_proof generation
- idempotency_key handling
- lease binding (when required)
- evidence attachment rules
- response envelope structure

This complexity warrants focused implementation and testing.

## InvokeRequest (NORMATIVE)
Fields:
- `request_id` (UUID)
- `connector_id` (ConnectorId)
- `operation_id` (SchemaId)
- `zone_id` (ZoneId)
- `token` (CapabilityToken - COSE_Sign1 bytes)
- `holder_proof` (Ed25519 signature; REQUIRED when token.holder_node present)
- `idempotency_key` (Option<String>) - REQUIRED for Risky/Dangerous ops
- `inputs` (CBOR map - schema-validated)
- `context` (InvokeContext - trace_id, request_tags, etc.)
- `lease_seq` (Option<u64>) - REQUIRED for singleton_writer connectors

### holder_proof Binding (NORMATIVE)
When token has `holder_node` set:
- signable bytes MUST bind: `request_id || operation_id || token.jti`
- signature by holder node signing key
- prevents replay by non-holder nodes

### Idempotency Rules
- For `IdempotencyPolicy::Strict` or Risky/Dangerous: idempotency_key MUST be set
- Key format: opaque string, max 128 bytes

## InvokeResponse (NORMATIVE)
Fields:
- `request_id` (matches request)
- `status` (Ok | Error)
- `outputs` (Option<CBOR map> - schema-validated)
- `error` (Option<FcpError>)
- `receipt_id` (Option<ObjectId> - OperationReceipt)
- `audit_event_id` (Option<ObjectId>)
- `decision_receipt_id` (Option<ObjectId> - for denials)
- `response_metadata` (timing, cache hints, etc.)

## Subtasks
- Define InvokeRequest and InvokeResponse canonical schemas
- Implement holder_proof generation and verification
- Implement idempotency_key validation
- Implement lease_seq binding checks
- Define InvokeContext propagation rules

## Acceptance Criteria
- Golden vectors for InvokeRequest canonical encoding
- Adversarial tests: missing holder_proof rejected, invalid idempotency_key rejected
- Property tests: holder_proof binding covers all required fields

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-308","title":"fcp.sentry: Performance monitoring data access","description":"# fcp.sentry: Performance monitoring data access (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose Sentry performance/trace surfaces so agents can:
- identify slow transactions and regressions
- correlate errors with performance degradation
- fetch trace/transaction summaries for RCA

## Scope
### Read operations
- Query transactions by project/environment/time range.
- Fetch a transaction/trace summary:
  - duration
  - spans breakdown
  - key tags
  - sample trace ids
- Fetch performance metrics trends (where available).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.performance.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Performance data may include URLs, user agents, and other sensitive metadata:
  - treat as tainted
  - avoid logging raw payloads

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.performance.transactions.query`
  - `sentry.performance.transaction.get`
  - `sentry.performance.trace.get_summary`
- Pagination helpers + time-window query helpers.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - query construction and pagination
  - normalization of key metrics
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff
  - taint propagation

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents can retrieve stable, comparable performance summaries for RCA.
- No secrets/PII leak through logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-31c7","title":"[FCP2] Offline Capability: Availability Tracking, OfflineAccess Metrics, Predictive Pre-staging","description":"# [FCP2] Offline Capability Tracking

## Goal
Implement the offline capability tracking from V2 spec section 21, enabling:
- Real-time availability tracking per object
- OfflineCapability/OfflineAccess metrics
- Predictive pre-staging based on user patterns
- Quantifiable offline resilience (SLO-ready)

## Normative Requirements (from FCP Specification V2 section 21)
- Nodes MUST track local symbol availability per object
- Nodes SHOULD support predictive pre-staging based on access patterns
- Offline resilience MUST be quantifiable (can_access, coverage ratio)

## Implementation Details

### OfflineCapability Schema
- accessible: HashMap<ObjectId, OfflineAccess>

### OfflineAccess Schema
- object_id: ObjectId
- local_symbols: usize (symbols held locally)
- k: usize (symbols needed for reconstruction)
- can_access(): bool (local_symbols >= k)
- coverage(): f64 (local_symbols / k)

### Predictive Pre-staging
- Track object access patterns per zone
- Pre-stage symbols for frequently accessed objects before needed
- Respect device storage constraints (DeviceProfile)
- Priority queue based on access frequency and recency

## Dependencies
- Requires Stores (1n78.15) for symbol tracking
- Requires DeviceProfile (wwq8) for storage constraints
- Integrates with Background Repair for coverage management

## Tests
- Unit tests: can_access(), coverage() calculations
- Integration tests: pre-staging triggers on access patterns
- E2E tests: offline read with reduced connectivity

## Acceptance Criteria
- Offline capability is queryable per object (can_access, coverage)
- Pre-staging improves offline availability for frequently accessed objects
- CLI reports offline readiness via fcp doctor --zone","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:40:47.286114824-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T02:39:21.448833959-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T02:39:21.448833959-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented OfflineAccess, OfflineCapability, AccessPatternTracker with 21 tests","labels":["fcp2","mvp","storage","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-31c7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:41:07.990459777-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-31c7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-wwq8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:41:08.131893453-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-36x3","title":"TEST: Revocation Unit/Adversarial Tests (Freshness, Chain Integrity, Scope Enforcement)","description":"# TEST: Revocation Unit/Adversarial Tests

## Goal
Prove that revocations are processed correctly and cannot be bypassed.

## Test Categories

### RevocationObject Parsing
- [ ] Valid revocation object parses
- [ ] Malformed CBOR rejected
- [ ] Missing required fields rejected
- [ ] Invalid scope rejected
- [ ] Owner signature verification succeeds/fails correctly

### Revocation Scopes
- [ ] Capability revocation: referenced capability becomes invalid
- [ ] IssuerKey revocation: tokens from that issuer become invalid
- [ ] NodeAttestation revocation: node operations become invalid
- [ ] ZoneKey revocation: triggers zone key rotation awareness
- [ ] ConnectorBinary revocation: connector cannot run

### RevocationEvent Chain
- [ ] Events hash-linked correctly (prev_hash binding)
- [ ] Monotonic seq enforced
- [ ] Duplicate seq rejected
- [ ] Gap in seq detected and flagged
- [ ] Out-of-order events handled correctly

### RevocationHead
- [ ] Quorum signatures required (n-f)
- [ ] Insufficient signatures rejected
- [ ] Signature from non-member rejected
- [ ] Signature set canonicalization (sorted by node_id)
- [ ] Multiple valid heads for same seq → fork detection

### Freshness Policy Enforcement
- [ ] Strict policy: stale revocation head → operation rejected
- [ ] Warn policy: stale head → operation allowed with degraded flag
- [ ] BestEffort policy: very stale head → operation allowed with audit
- [ ] max_age calculation correct
- [ ] Freshness check is O(1) (seq comparison, not chain traversal)

### RevocationRegistry
- [ ] Fast negative lookup (bloom filter or equivalent)
- [ ] False positives trigger full check
- [ ] True positive: revoked item correctly identified
- [ ] True negative: valid item passes quickly
- [ ] Registry update on new revocation event

### Attack Scenarios (Adversarial)
- [ ] Revocation withholding: node claims no revocations but others have them
- [ ] Revocation replay: old revocation re-submitted
- [ ] Revocation forgery: invalid owner signature rejected
- [ ] Stale frontier attack: verifier with very old revocation head
- [ ] Chain fork injection: two revocation heads for same seq detected

## Golden Vectors
- Valid revocation object bytes
- Valid revocation chain with multiple events
- RevocationHead with quorum signatures
- Bloom filter state after N revocations

## Fuzz Targets
- RevocationObject parsing
- RevocationEvent chain verification
- RevocationHead signature verification

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "revocation_scope": "Capability|IssuerKey|...",
  "target_id": "...",
  "chain_seq": 123,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "...",
  "freshness_policy": "Strict|Warn|BestEffort"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All revocation scopes tested
- Chain integrity verified
- Attack scenarios blocked
- Freshness policies enforced correctly

","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:08:44.640609726-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T17:23:02.474980801-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-15T17:23:02.474980801-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive revocation_golden_vectors.rs with:

- Chain integrity validation tests
- Quorum verification tests for RevocationHead
- Note: Tests compile but full verification blocked by pre-existing fcp-crypto rand_core version conflict","labels":["fcp2","mvp","revocation","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-36x3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:23:14.949469455-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-36x3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:23:44.814394636-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb.3","title":"CLI: fcp explain (DecisionReceipt renderer)","description":"# CLI: `fcp explain` (DecisionReceipt renderer)

## Goal
Implement `fcp explain --request <objectid>` to render the *mechanical evidence* behind an allow/deny.

## Behavior
- Load the DecisionReceipt for the request (or explain why it is missing).
- Render:
  - decision (allow/deny)
  - reason_code
  - evidence object ids and a short description of each (token, grants, checkpoint, approvals, policy head)
  - optional human message if present

## Output
- Human mode: readable explanation.
- `--json` mode: fully structured output for tooling.

## Tests
- Snapshot tests for representative deny reason codes.
- E2E: invoke a request that is denied and ensure `fcp explain` matches the DecisionReceipt.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can understand why a denial happened without guessing or disabling security.
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:00:31.845715273-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T10:32:53.990492605-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T10:32:53.990492605-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented fcp explain command with human-readable and JSON output modes. Includes types, rendering, validation, demo data for testing, and 18 unit tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:00:31.847133848-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:01:15.613157786-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:01:15.686931573-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:01:15.759025932-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:01:15.831961247-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb.4","title":"CLI: fcp audit tail (AuditEvent stream)","description":"# CLI: `fcp audit tail` (AuditEvent stream)

## Goal
Implement `fcp audit tail --zone <zone>` to tail audit events with trace correlation.

## Behavior
- Stream AuditEvent entries in order (by seq).
- Support filtering by:
  - connector_id
  - operation_id
  - correlation_id / trace_id
- Support `--json` output for piping.

## Tests
- E2E: generate audit events and assert tail shows them with stable ordering and required fields.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Audit output is usable for incident response without leaking secrets.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb.5","title":"CLI: fcp repair status (coverage + placement)","description":"# CLI: `fcp repair status` (coverage + placement)

## Goal
Implement `fcp repair status --zone <zone>` to report offline availability SLO health.

## Behavior
- Report coverage metrics (basis points):
  - distinct nodes
  - max_node_fraction_bps
  - coverage_bps
  - is_available
- Report last repair cycle actions and pending deficits.
- Support `--json` output for dashboards.

## Tests
- Simulation harness test: after removing symbols, status shows deficit; after repair cycle, deficit improves.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Offline availability is quantifiable and debuggable.

## Goal
Implement `fcp bench` as the user-facing runner for the platform benchmark suite.

## Behavior
- Run microbenchmarks for hot paths (CBOR, ObjectId, token verify, FCPS parse, RaptorQ decode, invoke-local/invoke-mesh).
- Emit machine-readable results (JSON) for regression tracking.
- Provide stable subcommands/flags so CI or users can run targeted benchmarks.

## Tests
- Snapshot tests for JSON result shape.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bench output is stable enough to track regressions over time.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-iqrb.7","title":"CLI: fcp new <name> (Connector scaffold + compliance precheck)","description":"# CLI: `fcp new <name>` (Connector scaffold + compliance precheck)

## Goal
Make it fast and safe to create a new **FCP2-compliant** connector crate by generating a correct skeleton (manifest + SDK surface + tests) and running mechanical prechecks.

## Context
We will build many connectors (Telegram, Discord, etc.). The highest leverage DX improvement is a generator that enforces:
- single-zone binding,
- default-deny NetworkConstraints,
- secretless operation by default (no secrets on disk; no logging secrets),
- structured JSON logging,
- unit test + E2E scaffolding,
- and a clear “what you must fill in” checklist.

This command is **developer tooling**. It must not weaken production security assumptions.

## Scope
In scope:
- Create a new connector crate with a minimal-but-correct FCP2 skeleton.
- Emit a default manifest with placeholder capabilities, sandbox profile, and NetworkConstraints.
- Emit placeholder operations and the correct receipts / error taxonomy wiring.
- Create deterministic unit test scaffolding and an E2E script skeleton.
- Run mechanical validation checks and print actionable diagnostics.

Out of scope:
- Any compatibility shim for legacy FCP1 protocols.
- Generating real credentials or storing secrets.

## Command UX (proposal)

### Basic usage
```bash
fcp new fcp.myservice
```

### Optional flags
- `--archetype request-response|streaming|bidirectional|polling|webhook|queue|file|database|cli|browser`
- `--zone z:project:<name>` (default: `z:project:default` or prompt)
- `--no-e2e` (if generating only unit test scaffolding)
- `--dry-run` (print planned files without writing)
- `--check` (validate an existing connector directory)

### Outputs
- Prints:
  - created file list
  - next steps
  - compliance precheck results (pass/fail with reasons)
- Supports `--json` output like the rest of `fcp-cli`.

## Skeleton contents (must generate)

### 1) Cargo crate
- Path: `connectors/<name>/`
- Library crate (or bin, depending on repo conventions) with:
  - minimal `Cargo.toml`
  - dependency on `fcp-sdk`
  - `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`

### 2) Manifest
Generate an initial FCP2 manifest that is:
- syntactically valid TOML
- references:
  - connector id
  - version
  - zone binding (single-zone)
  - capabilities list
  - sandbox profile
  - NetworkConstraints (default deny)

### 3) Operations + schemas
- Provide placeholder operations that demonstrate:
  - typed input/output schemas
  - error taxonomy usage (FCP-XXXX)
  - receipt emission
  - tracing fields (`correlation_id`, `zone_id`, `connector_id`, `operation_id`)

### 4) Tests
- Unit test scaffold:
  - a “happy path” operation test using a mock transport
  - negative tests for missing capability / denied NetworkConstraints
  - redaction test: assert secrets are never logged

- E2E scaffold:
  - a script or harness integration that:
    - installs the connector
    - invokes an operation
    - verifies DecisionReceipt / AuditEvent shape
    - exercises default-deny failure path

## Mechanical prechecks (must run)
At creation time (and for `--check`), run:
- Manifest validation
- Connector compliance checklist (structure + fields)
- NetworkConstraints default-deny invariant
- “no secrets on disk” lint (no examples with plaintext tokens)

These checks should reuse the platform’s mechanical compliance runner where possible.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `fcp new <name>` creates a connector skeleton that builds in the workspace.
- Generated connector starts V2-only and includes no FCP1 language or compatibility shims.
- Generated manifest includes single-zone binding + default-deny NetworkConstraints.
- Generated tests include at least:
  - happy path
  - default deny
  - redaction
- `fcp new --check` produces actionable diagnostics and supports `--json`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-iv4","title":"fcp.reddit: Messaging","description":"# fcp.reddit: Messaging (FCP2)

## Goal
Support Reddit inbox and direct messaging to enable:
- receiving replies/mentions
- sending messages to users (when permitted)

Messaging is privacy sensitive.

## Scope
- Fetch inbox (unread, mentions, messages) with pagination.
- Mark as read (optional; dangerous-ish).
- Send a message.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.messages.read`
- `reddit.messages.send` (dangerous)
- Optional: `reddit.messages.manage` (mark read)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - never log message bodies or usernames
- For sends:
  - receipts + audit events include recipient hash + message id

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.inbox.list`
  - `reddit.inbox.unread.list`
  - `reddit.message.send`
  - Optional: `reddit.inbox.mark_read`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - send request formation
  - redaction
  - audit/receipt on send

## Acceptance criteria
- Messaging operations are safe, auditable, and privacy-preserving.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.9.3","title":"TEST: Bitwarden Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only, redaction)","description":"# TEST: Bitwarden Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only, redaction)

## Goal
Mock-only tests for `fcp.bitwarden` with heavy emphasis on security:
- strict log redaction: secrets never appear (even on errors)
- no secrets on disk (guarded by tests)
- capability/approval gating for any dangerous reads/writes
- bounded response sizes and timeouts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests emit structured JSON logs and include negative cases.
- No real Bitwarden calls in unit tests.

## Goal
Run the shared connector compliance framework against `fcp.bitwarden`.

## Coverage
- default deny
- NetworkConstraints
- strict sandbox profile
- log redaction
- receipts/audit for any side effects

## Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence bundle produced and passes in CI.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lte5","title":"TEST: fcp-webhook Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-webhook Unit Test Suite

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for webhook handling library.

## Test Categories

### Signature Verification
- [ ] HMAC-SHA256 verification (Stripe, GitHub, etc.)
- [ ] Ed25519 signature verification
- [ ] Timestamp validation (replay attack prevention)
- [ ] Multiple signature schemes
- [ ] Invalid signature rejection

### Webhook Parsing
- [ ] JSON payload parsing
- [ ] Form-encoded payload parsing
- [ ] Content-Type handling
- [ ] Charset handling

### Event Routing
- [ ] Event type dispatch
- [ ] Wildcard subscriptions
- [ ] Event filtering

### Delivery Management
- [ ] Idempotency handling (deduplication)
- [ ] Event ordering guarantees
- [ ] Retry scheduling for failed processing
- [ ] Dead letter queue handling

### Security
- [ ] IP allowlist verification
- [ ] TLS certificate validation
- [ ] Request size limits
- [ ] Timeout enforcement

## Mocking Strategy
- Generate valid/invalid webhook payloads
- Mock signature generation with known keys
- Test various provider formats (GitHub, Stripe, Slack, etc.)

## Coverage Target: >95%","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:12:36.431123151-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T03:22:37.21072446-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-15T03:22:37.21072446-05:00","close_reason":"Webhook functionality is connector-specific, not a platform crate. Webhook testing belongs in individual connector test suites.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lte5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-vi7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T12:21:14.23447823-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-lv8h","title":"TEST: InvokeRequest/Response Unit Tests (Schema Validation, Holder Proof, Binding)","description":"# TEST: InvokeRequest/Response Unit Tests


## Goal
Prove InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse validation, binding rules, and holder proof verification are correct with golden vectors and fuzz resilience.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for the InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse types and their validation logic.

## Test Categories

### Schema Validation
- [ ] Valid InvokeRequest with all required fields
- [ ] Invalid InvokeRequest (missing operation_id) → rejection
- [ ] Invalid InvokeRequest (malformed arguments) → rejection
- [ ] Schema hash mismatch detection
- [ ] Argument type validation against operation schema

### Holder Proof Verification
- [ ] Valid holder_proof verifies correctly
- [ ] Missing holder_proof for risky operation → rejection
- [ ] Holder proof with wrong session key → rejection
- [ ] Holder proof replay detection
- [ ] Holder proof binding to request_id

### Binding Rules
- [ ] capability_token_id binding is enforced
- [ ] zone_id binding is checked
- [ ] connector_id binding is validated
- [ ] aud_binary binding for risky operations

### Response Validation
- [ ] Success response with valid result
- [ ] Error response with proper error taxonomy
- [ ] DecisionReceipt attached for denials
- [ ] Streaming response envelope handling

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/invoke/`
- `invoke_request_valid.cbor`
- `invoke_request_invalid_schema.cbor`
- `invoke_response_success.cbor`
- `invoke_response_denial.cbor`

## Logging Requirements
All tests emit structured JSON logs:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_invoke_schema_validation",
  "phase": "setup|execute|verify",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "operation_id": "...",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "evidence": {...}
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All InvokeRequest/Response validations are covered
- Golden vectors exist and match
- No panics on malformed inputs
- Fuzz target for request parsing exists

","notes":"Tests implemented (48 tests) in invoke_golden_vectors.rs by @OpusFCP. Covers schema validation, holder proof, binding rules, response validation, adversarial inputs. Tests pass - ready to close when 2vvy completes.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-m6u7","title":"fcp.microsoft365: Microsoft 365 Suite Integration Connector","description":"# fcp.microsoft365 - Microsoft 365 Suite Integration Connector (FCP2)

## Success Criteria
- `flywheel_connectors-m6u7.1` (Manifest + Capability Map) is complete and passes static validation (IDs, schemas, NetworkConstraints, safety tiers).
- `flywheel_connectors-m6u7.2` (Provisioning Automation) is complete, minimizes human prompts, and is fully mockable for tests.
- `flywheel_connectors-m6u7.3` (Unit/Integration Tests) are mock-only, include success + denial paths, and assert log redaction (no secrets/PII).
- `flywheel_connectors-m6u7.4` (E2E Compliance Run) passes the shared compliance runner and emits an evidence bundle (logs + receipts + audit checks).
- Connector is V2-only: single-zone binding, default deny, constrained egress, audited risky/dangerous ops.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 91/100** — High priority Tier 2 connector.

**Why Critical**: **50%+ of enterprise users are on Microsoft 365.** Cannot ignore for enterprise adoption. This connector provides feature parity with Google suite connectors and unlocks the enterprise productivity market.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Moderate Effort)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Bidirectional,  // Email send/receive
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,    // File/calendar CRUD
    ConnectorArchetype::Storage,        // OneDrive file storage
]
```
- **Bidirectional**: Outlook email, Teams messages
- **Operational**: Calendar, tasks, documents
- **Storage**: OneDrive/SharePoint files

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::SingletonWriter
```
- **SingletonWriter**: Delta sync tokens per resource
- State tracked: `{ delta_links: HashMap<Resource, DeltaLink>, sync_state: SyncState }`
- Lease required for writer fencing

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles OAuth tokens, PII data
- Memory isolation for credential protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Microsoft Graph API
    host_allow: vec\![
        "graph.microsoft.com".into(),
        "login.microsoftonline.com".into(),
        // For file uploads
        "*.sharepoint.com".into(),
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// Email content is external
email_content.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT
    | TaintFlags::PROMPT_SURFACE
    | TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;

// Attachments and URLs need verification
if content.contains_urls() || content.has_attachments() {
    content.provenance.taint |= TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;
}

// Calendar events may contain PII
calendar_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `m365.mail.read` | Risky | Email contains PII |
| `m365.calendar.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `m365.files.read` | Risky | May contain PII |
| `m365.users.read` | Risky | PII (directory) |
| `m365.mail.send` | Dangerous | External communication |
| `m365.files.write` | Risky | Modifies data |
| `m365.calendar.write` | Risky | Creates events |
| `m365.mail.delete` | Dangerous | Irreversible |
| `m365.files.delete` | Dangerous | Irreversible |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Sub-Connector Suite
This epic covers multiple integrated services:

| Sub-Connector | Description | Google Equivalent |
|--------------|-------------|-------------------|
| `fcp.outlook` | Email (rival to Gmail) | fcp.gmail |
| `fcp.onedrive` | File storage | fcp.gdrive |
| `fcp.excel-online` | Spreadsheets | fcp.gsheets |
| `fcp.word-online` | Documents | fcp.gdocs |
| `fcp.onenote` | Notes | N/A |
| `fcp.sharepoint` | Enterprise documents | N/A |
| `fcp.msplanner` | Task management | N/A |
| `fcp.teams` | Chat/collaboration | fcp.slack |

### Unified Microsoft Graph
All services accessed through **Microsoft Graph API** — one unified endpoint.

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Read my latest emails"
"Send an email with this report attached"
"Schedule a meeting with the team"
"Upload this file to OneDrive"
"Search SharePoint for the policy document"
"Create a task in Planner"
"Post to the team channel"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Slack Integration
**Cross-platform communication:**
- Teams ↔ Slack message bridging
- Unified notification routing

### Google Workspace Parity
- Feature equivalence for multi-cloud orgs
- Migration support

### Agent Mail Integration
- Email threads → agent mail
- Calendar notifications
- Task assignments

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **Microsoft Graph API**: Unified REST API
- **Delta queries**: Incremental sync
- **Webhooks (change notifications)**: Real-time updates

### Recommended Crates
- `graph-rs-sdk` — Microsoft Graph client
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `oauth2` — Authentication

### Authentication
| Mode | Use Case |
|------|----------|
| Delegated (OAuth 2.0) | User-context access |
| Application | Daemon/service access |
| Certificate | High-security apps |

### Scopes (Granular Permissions)
```
Mail.Read, Mail.Send
Calendars.ReadWrite
Files.ReadWrite.All
User.Read, User.ReadBasic.All
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:work:m365:full` | Full access | All services |
| `z:work:m365:mail` | Mail only | Outlook |
| `z:work:m365:files` | Files only | OneDrive/SharePoint |
| `z:work:m365:readonly` | Read-only | Safe operations |

### Security Considerations
- Email/files contain PII
- Audit all data access
- Scope permissions narrowly

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `bidirectional` — Email send/receive
- `operational` — Calendar, tasks, documents
- `storage` — File storage

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `graph.microsoft.com:443`
- `login.microsoftonline.com:443`
- `*.sharepoint.com:443`

### Credential Injection
- OAuth tokens via egress proxy
- Client credentials for app-only
- Refresh tokens managed securely

---

## Capability Model

### Mail Operations
- `m365.mail.read` — Read emails (Risky)
- `m365.mail.send` — Send emails (Dangerous)
- `m365.mail.delete` — Delete emails (Dangerous)

### Calendar Operations
- `m365.calendar.read` — Read events
- `m365.calendar.write` — Create/update events

### File Operations
- `m365.files.read` — Read files (Risky)
- `m365.files.write` — Upload/update files
- `m365.files.delete` — Delete files (Dangerous)

### Teams Operations
- `m365.teams.read` — Read messages
- `m365.teams.send` — Send messages (Dangerous)

---

## Operations (Representative)

### List Emails
```
GET /me/messages?$top=10&$orderby=receivedDateTime desc
```

### Send Email
```json
POST /me/sendMail
{
  "message": {
    "subject": "Subject",
    "body": {"contentType": "HTML", "content": "..."},
    "toRecipients": [{"emailAddress": {"address": "user@example.com"}}]
  }
}
```

### List Calendar Events
```
GET /me/calendar/events?$filter=start/dateTime ge '2024-01-01'
```

### Upload File
```
PUT /me/drive/root:/folder/file.txt:/content
Content-Type: text/plain

File content here
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Graph API request construction
- Response parsing
- Delta sync handling
- OAuth flow

### Integration Tests
- Mock Graph API responses
- Multi-service scenarios
- Error handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- PII handling audit

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Graph API calls in unit tests
- Email read/send functional
- Calendar CRUD works
- File upload/download works
- Delta sync implemented
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-m6u7.1","title":"fcp.microsoft365: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.microsoft365: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the Microsoft 365 connector manifest contract:
- archetypes (`operational`, `streaming`, `storage`, `knowledge`)
- operations + schema IDs by domain (mail/files/calendar/tasks)
- capability families + per-operation caps
- NetworkConstraints allowlist
- state model for delta tokens and webhook renewals

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete manifest spec stored here.
- Good/bad manifest examples exist for static compliance.
